III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 Steve Burke, a 26-year-old English man, won universal praise after returning a stolen mountain bike to its upset owner. One day, when Steve had just come home from the gym, he 21 by three men asking if he wanted the bike. Noticing a bike lock on the back wheel, Steve was immediately suspicious 22 its origin and realized that the bike was obviously stolen. However, he decided to buy it so that he could 23 its original owner and reunite it with its actual owner. After buying the bike, Steve posted a picture of it on social media and asked, “Has anyone had his or her bike stolen?” The post soon became popular with many people 24 and sharing it. Within hours, he was contacted by a woman claiming to know the original owner. She said this bike had belonged to a man 25 house had been broken into. Steve returned the bike to him because he had the key to the bike lock. Social media users were quick to praise Steve online. In addition, Halfords, the UK’s largest retailer of cycling products, also offered to give Steve a brand-new bike as a reward for his honesty and kindness.

What is green technology? It refers to any environment-friendly technology which can reduce our carbon footprint. Its primary objective is to reduce the harmful effects of human activities on the environment. Even though green technology is still in its beginnings, many exciting innovations have already been made in areas like renewable energy, recycling and waste management, as well as in everyday consumer products like electrical vehicles and smartphones. These new ideas or methods are sure to have a great impact on all aspects of our life. Here are 6 green technology innovations that are helping slow climate change.

questions.q: How many of the six green technologies are mentioned in the following passage? “Sam is a successful boss who has decided to go green. He works in a building grown with green leaves of climbing vine and drives an electric car to work every day. He seldom turns on an air-conditioner in his office, even when it is hot.”
options: 4. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 As more young, single Indians download dating apps on their smartphones, dating apps start to create some big changes for marriage in India. In the past, the majority of marriages in India were arranged by the parents. Nowadays, however, dating apps have gained great popularity rapidly. Foreign dating apps, including Tinder, Bumble, and Hinge, and local Indian apps, such as TrulyMadly and Aisle, are helping their users to pair up with partners of their choices. The youth of today hate the idea of arranged marriage and turn to dating apps to seek love and meet their potential partners online, not necessarily with the intention of marriage. Moreover, Indian parents are also learning to take a more liberal view of boy-girl relationships. It is believed that dating apps will break conventional barriers in India because dating apps follow a more liberal approach to relationships based on user’s shared values and lifestyles instead of religion or social class. Dating apps can connect men and women from different castes. In other words, dating apps not only allow people to meet their potential partners outside of their own social class but enable people who live in rural areas to look for their true love in cities. Dating apps are becoming the first place where people connect to get to know each other, and they are playing the role of modern-day cupids. Are there any other reasons that dating apps are becoming more popular in India? Perhaps, it is because of rising education and financial independence that well-educated women tend to make lifelong commitments, such as marriage, on their own. Many young women realize that a dating app is one of the best ways to meet their true love. What’s more, COVID-19 also encourages a shift to an increasingly online life, and it witnesses more young people investing their time in forming stronger emotional ties with their potential matches online.
